nativeRange.habitat: Coralline sand areas of clear lagoon and seaward reefs, at depths of 1–25 m

diet.notes: Has been documented feeding on small invertebrates and benthic organisms consistent with a trophic level of 3.4. In captivity tends to accept meaty frozen foods such as mysis shrimp, brine shrimp, and chopped seafood; smaller prey items are generally preferred.

avoidWith: dottybacks that may harass smaller gobies

summary: The Decorated Goby is a peaceful, sand-dwelling reef fish from the Indo-Pacific, found across clear lagoons and seaward reefs. It tends to do well in reef aquariums with a sandy substrate and similarly peaceful tankmates.
careNotes: The Decorated Goby (Istigobius decoratus) is a mid-sized benthic goby reaching around 13 cm, found across the Indo-West Pacific from the Persian Gulf and Red Sea east to Samoa, north to Taiwan, and south to Lord Howe Island. In the wild it tends to be found singly on coralline sand of clear lagoons and seaward reefs at depths of 1–25 m. A sandy substrate is strongly recommended in the aquarium, as the Decorated Goby commonly rests on and sifts through sand. It is generally considered easy to keep and tends to accept frozen meaty foods without difficulty. Standard reef water parameters — temperature 23–29°C, specific gravity 1.020–1.025, pH 8.1–8.4 — are generally adequate. Like most gobies, this species has a tendency to jump, so a well-fitted aquarium lid is advisable. The Decorated Goby tends to do best when kept singly or alongside dissimilar tankmates; conspecific aggression in confined spaces has been noted, particularly when sharing territory with other bottom-dwelling gobies.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Species-specific page confirmed. Provided care level (Easy), minimum tank size (20 gal / ~75 L), temperature (64–77°F / 17.8–25°C), specific gravity (1.020–1.025), pH (8.1–8.4), dKH (8–12), diet (carnivore: live/frozen brine shrimp, mysis shrimp, black worms, carnivore preparations), reef safe (Yes), temperament (Peaceful; territorial with conspecifics). Refugium strongly recommended as continuous live food source.

additional.notes: Confirmed scientific name, taxonomy, adult size (13.0 cm TL), native range (Indo-West Pacific: Persian Gulf/Red Sea to Samoa, north to Taiwan, south to Lord Howe Island), habitat (coralline sand, clear lagoon and seaward reefs, 1–25 m depth), temperature range (23–29°C — more conservative than LiveAquaria's 17.8–25°C; FishBase range used for waterParameters as it is ecologically derived and reef-appropriate), diet (carnivorous, trophic level 3.4), IUCN Least Concern.

additional.name: Quality Marine
additional.notes: Wholesale trade source. Confirmed minimum tank ~30 gal, temperature 72–78°F, salinity 1.020–1.025, pH 8.1–8.4, dKH 8–12, diet (frozen/live meaty foods), difficulty moderate-to-easy. Noted possible coral nipping behavior (minority finding; LiveAquaria and majority sources list reef safe Yes). May require live foods initially during acclimation.
